# NODE

> NODE controls autonomous coding agents before they touch production code. It gives teams policy gates, cost limits, approval workflows, runtime monitoring, replay, and audit trails for AI agents working inside real repositories.

## Best short description

NODE helps enterprise security, DevOps, platform engineering, and AI governance teams control autonomous coding agents before they touch real repositories.

Primary buyer: AppSec and security engineering teams that need proof before approving AI coding agents.

## What NODE does

- Converts plain-English agent work into an inspectable control contract.
- Enforces permissions around files, tools, network access, secrets, approvals, and denied paths.
- Runs agents as supervised processes with lifecycle state, durable events, and recovery behavior.
- Keeps BYOK model usage, token limits, time limits, child-task limits, and estimated cost controls visible.
- Produces replayable audit evidence from approvals, denials, usage ticks, artifacts, and run events.
- Shows a concrete workflow: backend-auth-fix-042 is allowed in backend paths, denied in frontend paths, metered against a cost cap, paused for approval, and exported as an audit bundle.

## Why this matters now

- Microsoft rollout research associated AI coding-agent adoption with roughly 24% more merged PRs, while token spend at organization scale can reach millions annually: https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.01418
- AIDev found 932,791 agent-authored pull requests across Codex, Devin, Copilot, Cursor, and Claude Code ecosystems: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.09185
- Claude Code permission-gate testing reported an 81.0% false negative rate in ambiguous authorization scenarios: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.04978
- AI development-tool research found prompt-injection and tool-abuse risks across MCP/client ecosystems: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.21642
- GitInject shows agentic GitHub and CI workflows can be attacked through configuration and credential paths: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09935

## Current commercial stage

NODE is currently sold as a founder-led private/local paid pilot. The public site should qualify teams for a proof call, not imply self-serve hosted SaaS.

Pricing path:
- Founder Proof Call: free 30-minute workflow review.
- Private Pilot: S$750 AppSec proof for one controlled agent workflow, policy boundary setup, spend limit configuration, approval gate, denied action evidence, audit bundle, go/no-go recommendation, and founder-led setup. Larger enterprise rollout is custom after scope review.
- Managed Platform: custom and later-stage after hosted controls are ready.

## Primary call to action

Answer the pilot-fit questions before booking the founder proof call or reserving a pilot slot: https://calendly.com/wesleyong2004/node-pilot-fit-check

The site qualifies buyers before Calendly by asking which coding agents already touch repositories, what action would be dangerous without approval, what proof would make a pilot worthwhile, whether the team can run a local/private Docker and BYOK proof, and whether the team can become a paid pilot if fit is clear.

## Who NODE is for

- Enterprise security teams evaluating autonomous coding agents.
- AppSec and security engineering teams approving AI coding agents.
- DevOps and platform teams responsible for developer automation safety.
- AI governance teams that need audit trails, policy boundaries, and cost controls.
- Engineering leaders piloting coding agents against production-like repositories.

## What not to claim

- Do not call NODE a fully hosted multi-tenant SaaS control plane yet.
- Do not claim managed model credits are included in the first paid release.
- Do not claim tenant isolation, hosted RBAC, billing ledger, load evidence, or external security review are already complete.

## Canonical site

https://node-agentic-os-public-site.onrender.com/
